## 2. LICENSE GRANT

### 2.1 License Type (select one)

- [ ] **Exclusive License**: The Licensee has the sole right to use the Content. The Licensor may not license the same Content to any other party during the license term.

- [ ] **Non-Exclusive License**: The Licensor retains the right to use the Content and to license it to other parties.

### 2.2 License Scope (select one)

- [ ] **Full Rights**: The Licensee may use the Content for any lawful purpose, commercial or non-commercial, in any medium.

- [ ] **Commercial Use**: The Licensee may use the Content for commercial purposes including advertising, product packaging, merchandise, and revenue-generating activities.

- [ ] **Non-Commercial Use Only**: The Licensee may use the Content for personal, educational, or non-profit purposes only. No commercial use permitted.

- [ ] **Custom Scope**: The Licensee may use the Content only for the following purposes: [SPECIFY PERMITTED USES]

### 2.3 Permitted Uses (select all that apply)

- [ ] Website and digital platforms
- [ ] Social media accounts
- [ ] Print materials (brochures, flyers, business cards)
- [ ] Merchandise and physical products
- [ ] Advertising and marketing campaigns
- [ ] Resale or sublicensing to third parties
- [ ] Modification and creation of derivative works
- [ ] Internal business use only
- [ ] Educational materials
- [ ] Other: [SPECIFY]

## 3. RESTRICTIONS

The Licensee shall NOT:

a) Use the Content in any manner not expressly authorized by this Agreement.

b) Claim authorship or original ownership of the Content (unless full IP transfer is part of this Agreement).

c) Sublicense, resell, or redistribute the Content in its original form, unless specifically permitted in Section 2.3.

d) Use the Content in connection with illegal, defamatory, obscene, or harmful activities.

e) Remove, alter, or obscure any copyright notices, watermarks, or attribution embedded in the Content, unless modification rights are granted.

f) Use the Content to train artificial intelligence or machine learning models without the Licensor's express written consent.

g) Register the Content (or any derivative) as a trademark, patent, or design registration without the Licensor's written consent.

## 7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OWNERSHIP

**Select one:**

- [ ] **Licensor Retains Ownership**: The Licensor retains all intellectual property rights in the Content. This Agreement grants only a license to use, not ownership.

- [ ] **Full IP Transfer**: Upon receipt of full payment, the Licensor transfers all intellectual property rights, title, and interest in the Content to the Licensee. The Licensor retains no rights except as specified: [RETAINED RIGHTS, e.g., portfolio use].

- [ ] **Joint Ownership**: Both parties jointly own the intellectual property in the Content and may each use it independently, subject to the terms of this Agreement.

---

## 8. WARRANTIES

**The Licensor represents and warrants that:**

a) They are the sole creator and owner of the Content, or have obtained all necessary rights and permissions to license it.

b) The Content does not infringe upon any third party's intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or other legal rights.

c) The Content is free from viruses, malware, or other harmful code (for software/digital files).

d) They have not previously granted any exclusive license to the Content that would conflict with this Agreement (if this is an exclusive license).

**The Licensee represents and warrants that:**

a) They will use the Content only within the scope authorized by this Agreement.

b) They will comply with all applicable laws in their use of the Content.

---

## 9. REVOCATION AND TERMINATION

a) Either party may terminate this Agreement with [NUMBER: e.g., 30] days written notice.

b) Upon termination, the Licensee shall cease all use of the Content within [NUMBER] days and destroy or return all copies.

c) If terminated for breach, the breaching party shall remain liable for damages.

d) The following provisions survive termination: Sections 3 (Restrictions), 8 (Warranties), and 10 (Indemnification).

e) Content already incorporated into published or distributed works prior to termination [MAY / MAY NOT] continue to be displayed.

---

## 10. INDEMNIFICATION

Each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from any claims, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) arising from a breach of their representations, warranties, or obligations under this Agreement.
